name: Redpanda
what_it_does: Kafka-compatible streaming platform written in C++ that delivers 10x lower latency than Kafka with no JVM dependency, available as self-hosted or managed cloud.
use_cases: Drop-in replacement for existing Kafka-based agent pipelines requiring lower latency without code changes
Produce and consume high-frequency events using standard Kafka client libraries with reduced infrastructure cost
Manage topics, ACLs, and cluster configuration via Redpanda Admin API for automated provisioning
Run Redpanda Connect (formerly Benthos) data pipelines to transform and route agent-generated events
Deploy lightweight single-node Redpanda in CI environments for integration testing Kafka-dependent agents
not_for: Workloads requiring Kafka's full MirrorMaker 2 replication topology across many clusters
Teams that need the broadest managed connector ecosystem — Confluent Cloud has more connectors
Environments where C++ binary deployment is operationally constrained
best_when: You need Kafka protocol compatibility but want significantly lower end-to-end latency and simpler operations without the JVM tuning overhead.
avoid_when: Your infrastructure team is deeply invested in Confluent Platform tooling and support contracts that do not cover Redpanda.

auth.notes: Redpanda Cloud uses API keys for Admin API; Kafka protocol auth uses SASL/SCRAM-SHA-256 or mTLS; ACLs control topic-level permissions per user principal.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Redpanda Cloud free tier: 1 cluster, limited throughput, 30-day trial credits; self-hosted community edition is free
pricing.paid_tiers: Redpanda Cloud from ~$0.10/GiB ingested; Dedicated clusters with reserved capacity from custom pricing
pricing.notes: Self-hosted Redpanda Community is BSL licensed (free for most uses); Enterprise license required for some advanced features.

performance.rate_limits: Admin API: 100 req/s default; Kafka protocol throughput limited by cluster tier; no artificial rate limits on self-hosted

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Kafka protocol errors surface through standard Kafka error codes which are well-documented; Admin API returns descriptive JSON errors; Kafka error code integers require lookup table.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Supports Kafka idempotent producer semantics (enable.idempotence=true); Admin API topic creation returns 400 on duplicate rather than being idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Redpanda uses Kafka protocol but not all Kafka admin client features map 1:1 — agents using newer AdminClient APIs may hit unsupported operation errors
Redpanda Connect pipelines (YAML-configured) are separate from the Kafka API — agents automating data pipelines must use the Connect HTTP API, not Kafka Admin API
Self-hosted Redpanda single-node mode disables replication — agents assuming RF>1 durability will silently lose data on node failure
The Admin API port (9644) is separate from the Kafka broker port (9092) — agents must track two endpoints per cluster
Topic configs set via Kafka AdminClient may differ from Redpanda-native defaults — always verify retention.ms and segment.bytes after creation

security_score_components.security_notes: No JVM removes a large attack surface; TLS enforced in cloud, opt-in self-hosted; SASL/SCRAM and mTLS both supported; BSL license requires review for commercial use cases.
